concepts.label: Electric Motor
concepts.definition: A machine that converts electrical energy into mechanical motion through electromagnetic induction

concepts.label: AC Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor driven by alternating current, commonly used in industrial applications

concepts.label: DC Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor that runs on direct current, offering precise speed control

concepts.label: Stepper Motor
concepts.definition: A brushless DC motor that divides full rotation into discrete steps for precise positioning

concepts.label: Servo Motor
concepts.definition: A rotary actuator that allows for precise control of angular position, velocity and acceleration

concepts.label: Induction Motor
concepts.definition: An AC motor where electric current in the rotor is induced by rotating magnetic field

concepts.label: Synchronous Motor
concepts.definition: An AC motor where rotor rotation is synchronized with frequency of supply current

concepts.label: Linear Motor
concepts.definition: An electric motor that produces motion in a straight line rather than rotational motion

concepts.label: Brushless DC Motor
concepts.definition: A synchronous motor using electronic commutation instead of brushes for higher efficiency

concepts.label: Universal Motor
concepts.definition: A commutated series-wound motor that can operate on AC or DC supply

concepts.label: Transistor
concepts.definition: A semiconductor device used to amplify or switch electronic signals

concepts.label: BJT
concepts.definition: Bipolar Junction Transistor - a three-terminal device using both electrons and holes

concepts.label: MOSFET
concepts.definition: Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor for voltage-controlled switching

concepts.label: JFET
concepts.definition: Junction Field-Effect Transistor with high input impedance and voltage control

concepts.label: IGBT
concepts.definition: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor combining MOSFET and BJT characteristics

concepts.label: Operational Amplifier
concepts.definition: High-gain voltage amplifier with differential input and single-ended output

concepts.label: Comparator
concepts.definition: A circuit that compares two voltages and outputs a digital signal

concepts.label: Voltage Regulator
concepts.definition: A circuit designed to maintain a constant voltage level

concepts.label: Diode
concepts.definition: A semiconductor device that allows current flow in only one direction

concepts.label: LED
concepts.definition: Light Emitting Diode - a semiconductor that emits light when current passes through

concepts.label: Resistor
concepts.definition: A passive component that opposes the flow of electric current

concepts.label: Capacitor
concepts.definition: A passive component that stores electrical energy in an electric field

concepts.label: Inductor
concepts.definition: A passive component that stores energy in a magnetic field when current flows through it

concepts.label: Transformer
concepts.definition: A device that transfers electrical energy between circuits through electromagnetic induction

concepts.label: Relay
concepts.definition: An electrically operated switch using electromagnetic coil to control contacts

concepts.label: Fuse
concepts.definition: A safety device that melts to break circuit when current exceeds safe levels

concepts.label: Circuit Breaker
concepts.definition: An automatic electrical switch designed to protect circuits from damage

concepts.label: Printed Circuit Board
concepts.definition: A board that mechanically supports and electrically connects electronic components

concepts.label: IC
concepts.definition: Integrated Circuit - a semiconductor containing multiple interconnected electronic components

concepts.label: Microcontroller
concepts.definition: A small computer on a single integrated circuit containing processor, memory and peripherals

concepts.label: CPU
concepts.definition: Central Processing Unit - the primary component that executes instructions in a computer

concepts.label: GPU
concepts.definition: Graphics Processing Unit - specialized processor designed for parallel graphics computations

concepts.label: RAM
concepts.definition: Random Access Memory - volatile memory for temporary data storage during processing

concepts.label: ROM
concepts.definition: Read-Only Memory - non-volatile memory typically containing firmware or boot instructions

concepts.label: SSD
concepts.definition: Solid State Drive - data storage device using integrated circuit assemblies

concepts.label: HDD
concepts.definition: Hard Disk Drive - data storage using magnetic storage on rotating disks

concepts.label: Cache Memory
concepts.definition: High-speed memory buffer between CPU and main memory for faster access

concepts.label: Motherboard
concepts.definition: Main circuit board connecting all computer components together

concepts.label: Power Supply Unit
concepts.definition: Device that converts AC mains power to regulated DC power for computer components

concepts.label: Heat Sink
concepts.definition: Passive heat exchanger that transfers heat from electronic components to air

concepts.label: Network Interface Card
concepts.definition: Hardware component that connects computer to network

concepts.label: Ethernet
concepts.definition: Family of wired networking technologies for local area networks

concepts.label: WiFi
concepts.definition: Wireless networking technology using radio waves for local area connectivity

concepts.label: Bluetooth
concepts.definition: Short-range wireless technology for connecting devices over short distances

concepts.label: Router
concepts.definition: Networking device that forwards data packets between computer networks

concepts.label: Switch
concepts.definition: Networking device that connects devices on network by using MAC addresses

concepts.label: Firewall
concepts.definition: Network security device that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing traffic

concepts.label: Load Balancer
concepts.definition: Device that distributes network traffic across multiple servers

concepts.label: Modem
concepts.definition: Device that modulates and demodulates signals for data transmission

concepts.label: Antenna
concepts.definition: Device for transmitting or receiving electromagnetic waves

concepts.label: Internal Combustion Engine
concepts.definition: Heat engine where combustion of fuel occurs within engine cylinder

concepts.label: Four Stroke Engine
concepts.definition: Internal combustion engine with intake, compression, power and exhaust strokes

concepts.label: Two Stroke Engine
concepts.definition: Internal combustion engine completing power cycle in two piston strokes

concepts.label: Diesel Engine
concepts.definition: Compression ignition engine using diesel fuel for combustion

concepts.label: Gasoline Engine
concepts.definition: Spark ignition engine using gasoline as fuel

concepts.label: Turbocharger
concepts.definition: Forced induction device that increases engine power using exhaust gases

concepts.label: Supercharger
concepts.definition: Mechanically driven forced induction device for increasing engine power

concepts.label: Carburetor
concepts.definition: Device that mixes air and fuel for internal combustion engines

concepts.label: Fuel Injection
concepts.definition: System that delivers fuel into combustion chamber or intake manifold

concepts.label: Catalytic Converter
concepts.definition: Emission control device that reduces toxic gases in exhaust

concepts.label: Manual Transmission
concepts.definition: Multi-speed transmission where gear changes are made manually by driver

concepts.label: Automatic Transmission
concepts.definition: Multi-speed transmission that changes gears automatically

concepts.label: CVT
concepts.definition: Continuously Variable Transmission providing seamless range of gear ratios

concepts.label: Differential
concepts.definition: Gear system allowing wheels to rotate at different speeds while cornering

concepts.label: Clutch
concepts.definition: Mechanical device for engaging and disengaging engine from transmission

concepts.label: Torque Converter
concepts.definition: Fluid coupling device for transferring rotating power from engine to transmission

concepts.label: Planetary Gear
concepts.definition: Gear system with central sun gear surrounded by planet gears

concepts.label: Worm Gear
concepts.definition: Gear arrangement with worm screw meshing with worm wheel

concepts.label: Helical Gear
concepts.definition: Cylindrical gear with teeth cut at angle to axis for smooth operation

concepts.label: Bevel Gear
concepts.definition: Conical gear for transmitting power between intersecting shafts

concepts.label: Hydraulic System
concepts.definition: Power transmission system using pressurized liquid to generate mechanical force

concepts.label: Hydraulic Pump
concepts.definition: Mechanical device that converts mechanical power into hydraulic energy

concepts.label: Hydraulic Cylinder
concepts.definition: Linear actuator powered by pressurized hydraulic fluid

concepts.label: Hydraulic Valve
concepts.definition: Device for controlling direction, pressure, and flow rate of hydraulic fluid

concepts.label: Pneumatic System
concepts.definition: Power transmission system using compressed air to generate mechanical force

concepts.label: Air Compressor
concepts.definition: Machine that converts power into potential energy stored in pressurized air

concepts.label: Pneumatic Cylinder
concepts.definition: Linear actuator powered by compressed air

concepts.label: Pressure Relief Valve
concepts.definition: Safety device that relieves pressure when it exceeds set limit

concepts.label: Accumulator
concepts.definition: Energy storage device that stores fluid under pressure

concepts.label: Filter
concepts.definition: Device for removing contaminants from fluid systems

concepts.label: Bearing
concepts.definition: Machine element that constrains relative motion between moving parts

concepts.label: Ball Bearing
concepts.definition: Rolling element bearing using balls to maintain separation between races

concepts.label: Roller Bearing
concepts.definition: Rolling element bearing using cylindrical rollers instead of balls

concepts.label: Thrust Bearing
concepts.definition: Bearing designed to handle axial loads rather than radial loads

concepts.label: Journal Bearing
concepts.definition: Plain bearing that supports rotating shaft in radial direction

concepts.label: Seal
concepts.definition: Device that prevents leakage between moving and stationary parts

concepts.label: O-Ring
concepts.definition: Circular sealing element with round cross-section for static and dynamic sealing

concepts.label: Gasket
concepts.definition: Mechanical seal filling space between two objects to prevent leakage

concepts.label: Spring
concepts.definition: Elastic object that stores mechanical energy when compressed or extended

concepts.label: Coil Spring
concepts.definition: Helical spring wound in coil form for compression or tension applications

concepts.label: Solar Panel
concepts.definition: Device that converts sunlight into electricity using photovoltaic effect

concepts.label: Solar Cell
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts light energy directly into electrical energy

concepts.label: Wind Turbine
concepts.definition: Device that converts kinetic energy from wind into electrical energy

concepts.label: Wind Generator
concepts.definition: Electrical generator driven by wind turbine for producing electricity

concepts.label: Hydroelectric Generator
concepts.definition: Generator powered by flowing water to produce electricity

concepts.label: Nuclear Reactor
concepts.definition: Device to initiate and control sustained nuclear chain reaction for energy

concepts.label: Steam Turbine
concepts.definition: Turbine driven by steam pressure to generate rotational mechanical energy

concepts.label: Gas Turbine
concepts.definition: Turbine engine using combustion gases to drive compressor and generate power

concepts.label: Generator
concepts.definition: Device that converts mechanical energy into electrical energy

concepts.label: Alternator
concepts.definition: AC generator using electromagnetic induction to produce alternating current

concepts.label: Battery
concepts.definition: Device that stores electrical energy and releases it as electric current

concepts.label: Lithium Ion Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery where lithium ions move between anode and cathode

concepts.label: Lead Acid Battery
concepts.definition: Rechargeable battery using lead and sulfuric acid for automotive applications

concepts.label: Fuel Cell
concepts.definition: Device that generates electricity through chemical reaction of hydrogen and oxygen

concepts.label: Capacitor Bank
concepts.definition: Group of capacitors connected to store large amounts of electrical energy

concepts.label: Flywheel
concepts.definition: Rotating mechanical device for storing kinetic energy

concepts.label: Inverter
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts DC power to AC power

concepts.label: Rectifier
concepts.definition: Electronic device that converts AC power to DC power

concepts.label: UPS
concepts.definition: Uninterruptible Power Supply providing backup power during outages

concepts.label: Power Grid
concepts.definition: Interconnected network for delivering electricity from producers to consumers

concepts.label: Concrete
concepts.definition: Composite material composed of cement, water, aggregates and admixtures

concepts.label: Steel
concepts.definition: Alloy of iron and carbon with improved strength and durability

concepts.label: Reinforced Concrete
concepts.definition: Concrete strengthened with steel rebar to handle tension and compression

concepts.label: Prestressed Concrete
concepts.definition: Concrete compressed by tensioned cables before loading to improve performance

concepts.label: Rebar
concepts.definition: Steel reinforcement bar used to strengthen concrete in tension

concepts.label: Beam
concepts.definition: Structural element that withstands loads primarily by resisting bending

concepts.label: Column
concepts.definition: Vertical structural element designed to transmit compressive loads

concepts.label: Foundation
concepts.definition: Lower portion of structure that transfers loads to the ground

concepts.label: Truss
concepts.definition: Framework of beams forming triangular units for structural strength

concepts.label: Arch
concepts.definition: Curved structural element spanning opening and supporting load above

concepts.label: Welding
concepts.definition: Process of joining materials by melting and fusing them together

concepts.label: Arc Welding
concepts.definition: Welding process using electric arc to melt and join metals

concepts.label: TIG Welding
concepts.definition: Tungsten Inert Gas welding using tungsten electrode and inert gas shielding

concepts.label: MIG Welding
concepts.definition: Metal Inert Gas welding using consumable wire electrode and gas shielding

concepts.label: Soldering
concepts.definition: Process of joining metals using molten filler metal at low temperatures

concepts.label: Brazing
concepts.definition: Metal joining process using molten filler metal at temperatures above 450°C

concepts.label: Machining
concepts.definition: Manufacturing process removing material to create desired shape and finish

concepts.label: CNC Machine
concepts.definition: Computer Numerical Control machine for automated machining operations

concepts.label: Lathe
concepts.definition: Machine tool for rotating workpiece while cutting tool shapes the material

concepts.label: Milling Machine
concepts.definition: Machine tool using rotating cutters to remove material from workpiece

concepts.label: 3D Printer
concepts.definition: Additive manufacturing device creating objects by depositing material layer by layer

concepts.label: FDM Printing
concepts.definition: Fused Deposition Modeling - 3D printing by extruding thermoplastic filament

concepts.label: SLA Printing
concepts.definition: Stereolithography - 3D printing using laser to cure photopolymer resin

concepts.label: SLS Printing
concepts.definition: Selective Laser Sintering - 3D printing by sintering powder with laser

concepts.label: CAD
concepts.definition: Computer Aided Design - software for creating technical drawings and models

concepts.label: CAM
concepts.definition: Computer Aided Manufacturing - software for generating machine tool paths

concepts.label: CAE
concepts.definition: Computer Aided Engineering - software for analysis and simulation

concepts.label: FEA
concepts.definition: Finite Element Analysis - numerical method for solving engineering problems

concepts.label: CFD
concepts.definition: Computational Fluid Dynamics - numerical analysis of fluid flow problems

concepts.label: Rapid Prototyping
concepts.definition: Quick fabrication of physical parts using additive manufacturing

concepts.label: Robot
concepts.definition: Programmable machine capable of carrying out complex actions automatically

concepts.label: Industrial Robot
concepts.definition: Automatically controlled reprogrammable robot used in manufacturing

concepts.label: Robotic Arm
concepts.definition: Multi-jointed manipulator designed to perform various tasks

concepts.label: End Effector
concepts.definition: Device attached to robot arm for interacting with environment

concepts.label: Gripper
concepts.definition: End effector designed to grasp and manipulate objects

concepts.label: Sensor
concepts.definition: Device that detects and responds to physical input from environment

concepts.label: Proximity Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor that detects presence of nearby objects without contact

concepts.label: Pressure Sensor
concepts.definition: Device that measures pressure of gases or liquids

concepts.label: Temperature Sensor
concepts.definition: Device that measures temperature and converts it to electrical signal

concepts.label: Position Sensor
concepts.definition: Device that measures position or displacement of object

concepts.label: Encoder
concepts.definition: Sensor that converts motion into electrical signals for position feedback

concepts.label: Absolute Encoder
concepts.definition: Encoder that provides unique position value for each shaft position

concepts.label: Incremental Encoder
concepts.definition: Encoder that provides relative position information from reference point

concepts.label: Resolver
concepts.definition: Rotary transformer used for measuring degrees of rotation

concepts.label: LVDT
concepts.definition: Linear Variable Differential Transformer for measuring linear displacement

concepts.label: Strain Gauge
concepts.definition: Sensor for measuring strain or deformation in materials

concepts.label: Load Cell
concepts.definition: Transducer that converts force into measurable electrical output

concepts.label: Accelerometer
concepts.definition: Device that measures acceleration forces acting on object

concepts.label: Gyroscope
concepts.definition: Device for measuring or maintaining orientation based on angular momentum

concepts.label: Magnetometer
concepts.definition: Instrument for measuring magnetic field strength and direction

concepts.label: PLC
concepts.definition: Programmable Logic Controller for industrial automation and control

concepts.label: HMI
concepts.definition: Human Machine Interface for operator interaction with machines

concepts.label: SCADA
concepts.definition: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system for monitoring processes

concepts.label: DCS
concepts.definition: Distributed Control System for controlling distributed processes

concepts.label: PID Controller
concepts.definition: Proportional-Integral-Derivative controller for process control

concepts.label: Feedback Control
concepts.definition: Control system using output measurement to correct system behavior

concepts.label: Open Loop Control
concepts.definition: Control system without feedback from output to input

concepts.label: Closed Loop Control
concepts.definition: Control system using feedback to automatically correct output

concepts.label: Actuator
concepts.definition: Component that moves or controls mechanism or system

concepts.label: Linear Actuator
concepts.definition: Actuator that creates motion in straight line

concepts.label: Machine Vision
concepts.definition: Technology for providing imaging-based automatic inspection and analysis

concepts.label: Computer Vision
concepts.definition: Field of AI enabling computers to interpret visual information

concepts.label: Image Processing
concepts.definition: Analysis and manipulation of digital images using algorithms

concepts.label: OCR
concepts.definition: Optical Character Recognition - converting text images to machine-readable text

concepts.label: Barcode Scanner
concepts.definition: Optical scanner for reading printed barcodes

concepts.label: QR Code
concepts.definition: Two-dimensional barcode for storing information readable by imaging devices

concepts.label: RFID
concepts.definition: Radio Frequency Identification using electromagnetic fields to identify objects

concepts.label: NFC
concepts.definition: Near Field Communication for short-range wireless data exchange

concepts.label: GPS
concepts.definition: Global Positioning System providing location and time information

concepts.label: LIDAR
concepts.definition: Light Detection and Ranging using laser pulses to measure distances

concepts.label: TCP/IP
concepts.definition: Transmission Control Protocol/Internet Protocol - fundamental internet communication

concepts.label: HTTP
concepts.definition: HyperText Transfer Protocol for transferring web content

concepts.label: HTTPS
concepts.definition: Secure HTTP using encryption for safe web communication

concepts.label: DNS
concepts.definition: Domain Name System translating domain names to IP addresses

concepts.label: DHCP
concepts.definition: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for automatic IP address assignment

concepts.label: FTP
concepts.definition: File Transfer Protocol for transferring files over network

concepts.label: SSH
concepts.definition: Secure Shell protocol for secure remote access and command execution

concepts.label: VPN
concepts.definition: Virtual Private Network creating secure connection over public network

concepts.label: API
concepts.definition: Application Programming Interface defining how software components interact

concepts.label: REST API
concepts.definition: Representational State Transfer API using HTTP methods for web services

concepts.label: Database
concepts.definition: Organized collection of structured information stored electronically

concepts.label: SQL
concepts.definition: Structured Query Language for managing relational databases

concepts.label: NoSQL
concepts.definition: Non-relational database systems for handling unstructured data

concepts.label: Cloud Computing
concepts.definition: Delivery of computing services over internet including storage and processing

concepts.label: Server
concepts.definition: Computer system providing services to other computers over network

concepts.label: Virtual Machine
concepts.definition: Software emulation of computer system running on physical hardware

concepts.label: Container
concepts.definition: Lightweight virtualization packaging application with dependencies

concepts.label: Microservices
concepts.definition: Architectural approach using small, independent services for applications

concepts.label: Load Balancing
concepts.definition: Distribution of network traffic across multiple servers for performance

concepts.label: CDN
concepts.definition: Content Delivery Network distributing content across geographic locations

concepts.label: Artificial Intelligence
concepts.definition: Computer systems able to perform tasks that typically require human intelligence

concepts.label: Machine Learning
concepts.definition: AI technique enabling computers to learn without explicit programming

concepts.label: Neural Network
concepts.definition: Computing system inspired by biological neural networks for pattern recognition

concepts.label: Deep Learning
concepts.definition: Machine learning using artificial neural networks with multiple layers

concepts.label: Algorithm
concepts.definition: Step-by-step procedure for solving problem or completing task

concepts.label: Data Mining
concepts.definition: Process of discovering patterns in large datasets using computational methods

concepts.label: Big Data
concepts.definition: Extremely large datasets requiring special tools and techniques for processing

concepts.label: IoT
concepts.definition: Internet of Things - network of physical devices connected to internet

concepts.label: Edge Computing
concepts.definition: Distributed computing bringing computation closer to data sources

concepts.label: Blockchain
concepts.definition: Distributed ledger technology using cryptographic hashing for data integrity

concepts.label: Satellite
concepts.definition: Artificial object orbiting Earth for communication or observation

concepts.label: Satellite Communication
concepts.definition: Wireless communication using satellites as relay stations

concepts.label: Geostationary Orbit
concepts.definition: Orbit where satellite remains above same point on Earth's surface

concepts.label: Low Earth Orbit
concepts.definition: Orbit close to Earth's surface typically below 2000 km altitude

concepts.label: Radio Frequency
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation frequency range used for wireless communication

concepts.label: Microwave
concepts.definition: High frequency electromagnetic radiation used for communication and heating

concepts.label: Fiber Optic Cable
concepts.definition: High-speed data transmission medium using light pulses through glass fibers

concepts.label: Coaxial Cable
concepts.definition: Electrical cable with central conductor surrounded by insulation and shield

concepts.label: Twisted Pair Cable
concepts.definition: Cable with pairs of insulated copper wires twisted together

concepts.label: Cellular Network
concepts.definition: Wireless network using cell towers for mobile communication coverage

concepts.label: 5G Network
concepts.definition: Fifth generation cellular network technology with high speed and low latency

concepts.label: 4G LTE
concepts.definition: Fourth generation Long Term Evolution cellular technology

concepts.label: WiFi 6
concepts.definition: Sixth generation wireless networking standard with improved efficiency

concepts.label: Bandwidth
concepts.definition: Range of frequencies or data transfer capacity of communication channel

concepts.label: Latency
concepts.definition: Time delay between transmission and reception of data

concepts.label: Throughput
concepts.definition: Rate of successful message delivery over communication channel

concepts.label: Packet Switching
concepts.definition: Method of data transmission dividing data into packets for network routing

concepts.label: Circuit Switching
concepts.definition: Communication method establishing dedicated connection for data transmission

concepts.label: Quality of Service
concepts.definition: Network performance metrics ensuring acceptable service levels

concepts.label: Network Topology
concepts.definition: Physical or logical arrangement of network elements and connections

concepts.label: Radar
concepts.definition: Detection system using radio waves to determine range and speed of objects

concepts.label: Sonar
concepts.definition: Sound navigation and ranging using acoustic waves underwater

concepts.label: Ultrasound
concepts.definition: Sound waves above human hearing range used for imaging and measurement

concepts.label: X-Ray
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation for medical imaging and material inspection

concepts.label: CT Scanner
concepts.definition: Computed Tomography using X-rays for cross-sectional body imaging

concepts.label: MRI
concepts.definition: Magnetic Resonance Imaging using magnetic fields for detailed body imaging

concepts.label: Laser
concepts.definition: Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation producing coherent light

concepts.label: Fiber Laser
concepts.definition: Laser using optical fiber doped with rare-earth elements as gain medium

concepts.label: Diode Laser
concepts.definition: Semiconductor laser using p-n junction for light emission

concepts.label: Gas Laser
concepts.definition: Laser using gas as gain medium for light amplification

concepts.label: Photovoltaic Cell
concepts.definition: Device converting light energy directly into electrical energy

concepts.label: Silicon Solar Cell
concepts.definition: Photovoltaic cell made from silicon semiconductor material

concepts.label: Thin Film Solar Cell
concepts.definition: Photovoltaic cell made by depositing thin layers of photovoltaic material

concepts.label: Concentrated Solar Power
concepts.definition: Solar thermal technology using mirrors to concentrate sunlight for heat generation

concepts.label: Solar Inverter
concepts.definition: Device converting DC power from solar panels to AC power for grid use

concepts.label: Maximum Power Point Tracking
concepts.definition: Algorithm to optimize power output from solar panels

concepts.label: Net Metering
concepts.definition: Billing arrangement crediting solar system owners for excess electricity

concepts.label: Grid Tie Inverter
concepts.definition: Inverter connecting solar system to electrical grid

concepts.label: Off Grid System
concepts.definition: Solar power system operating independently from electrical grid

concepts.label: Battery Storage System
concepts.definition: System storing electrical energy for later use in renewable energy applications

concepts.label: Wind Farm
concepts.definition: Group of wind turbines generating electricity at same location

concepts.label: Horizontal Axis Wind Turbine
concepts.definition: Wind turbine with rotor axis parallel to ground

concepts.label: Vertical Axis Wind Turbine
concepts.definition: Wind turbine with rotor axis perpendicular to ground

concepts.label: Wind Speed Sensor
concepts.definition: Device measuring wind velocity for turbine control and monitoring

concepts.label: Pitch Control
concepts.definition: System adjusting blade angle to optimize power output and protect turbine

concepts.label: Yaw System
concepts.definition: Mechanism orienting wind turbine rotor to face wind direction

concepts.label: Gearbox
concepts.definition: Mechanical device increasing rotational speed from rotor to generator

concepts.label: Direct Drive Generator
concepts.definition: Wind turbine generator directly connected to rotor without gearbox

concepts.label: Power Curve
concepts.definition: Graph showing wind turbine power output versus wind speed

concepts.label: Cut-in Speed
concepts.definition: Minimum wind speed at which turbine starts generating power

concepts.label: Thermal Power Plant
concepts.definition: Power station using heat to generate steam for electricity production

concepts.label: Combined Cycle Power Plant
concepts.definition: Power plant using gas and steam turbines for higher efficiency

concepts.label: Cogeneration
concepts.definition: Simultaneous production of electricity and useful heat from same energy source

concepts.label: Heat Exchanger
concepts.definition: Device transferring heat between two or more fluids

concepts.label: Boiler
concepts.definition: Pressure vessel generating steam by heating water using fuel combustion

concepts.label: Condenser
concepts.definition: Device converting steam back to liquid water in power plants

concepts.label: Cooling Tower
concepts.definition: Structure for removing waste heat from power plant cooling water

concepts.label: Rankine Cycle
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic cycle used in steam power plants for energy conversion

concepts.label: Brayton Cycle
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic cycle used in gas turbines for power generation

concepts.label: Otto Cycle
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic cycle used in gasoline internal combustion engines

concepts.label: Nuclear Fission
concepts.definition: Nuclear reaction splitting atomic nucleus into smaller fragments releasing energy

concepts.label: Nuclear Fuel
concepts.definition: Fissile material used in nuclear reactor to sustain nuclear reactions

concepts.label: Control Rod
concepts.definition: Device containing neutron-absorbing material for controlling nuclear reaction rate

concepts.label: Reactor Core
concepts.definition: Central part of nuclear reactor containing fuel assemblies

concepts.label: Pressurized Water Reactor
concepts.definition: Nuclear reactor using pressurized water as coolant and moderator

concepts.label: Boiling Water Reactor
concepts.definition: Nuclear reactor where water boils in reactor core to generate steam

concepts.label: Moderator
concepts.definition: Material slowing down neutrons to sustain nuclear chain reaction

concepts.label: Coolant
concepts.definition: Fluid removing heat from nuclear reactor core

concepts.label: Containment Structure
concepts.definition: Barrier designed to prevent release of radioactive materials

concepts.label: Nuclear Waste
concepts.definition: Radioactive waste material from nuclear power generation

concepts.label: Electric Vehicle
concepts.definition: Vehicle using electric motors powered by rechargeable battery packs

concepts.label: Hybrid Electric Vehicle
concepts.definition: Vehicle combining internal combustion engine with electric motor

concepts.label: Plug-in Hybrid
concepts.definition: Hybrid vehicle with larger battery rechargeable from external power source

concepts.label: Regenerative Braking
concepts.definition: System recovering energy during braking to recharge vehicle battery

concepts.label: Electric Vehicle Charging
concepts.definition: Process of supplying electrical energy to recharge electric vehicle battery

concepts.label: Fast Charging
concepts.definition: High-power charging system for rapid electric vehicle battery charging

concepts.label: Battery Management System
concepts.definition: Electronic system monitoring and managing rechargeable battery pack

concepts.label: Electric Motor Controller
concepts.definition: Device controlling speed and torque of electric motor in vehicles

concepts.label: Traction Battery
concepts.definition: High-voltage battery pack providing power to electric vehicle motor

concepts.label: Range Extender
concepts.definition: Small generator extending electric vehicle range when battery depletes

concepts.label: Automotive Engine Management
concepts.definition: Electronic system controlling various engine functions for optimal performance

concepts.label: ECU
concepts.definition: Electronic Control Unit managing various automotive systems

concepts.label: CAN Bus
concepts.definition: Controller Area Network for communication between automotive electronic systems

concepts.label: OBD
concepts.definition: On-Board Diagnostics system for vehicle self-diagnostic and reporting

concepts.label: Anti-lock Braking System
concepts.definition: Safety system preventing wheels from locking during braking

concepts.label: Electronic Stability Control
concepts.definition: System improving vehicle stability by detecting and reducing loss of traction

concepts.label: Traction Control System
concepts.definition: System preventing wheel spin by reducing engine power or applying brakes

concepts.label: Airbag System
concepts.definition: Passive safety system deploying inflatable cushions during collision

concepts.label: Cruise Control
concepts.definition: System automatically maintaining constant vehicle speed

concepts.label: Adaptive Cruise Control
concepts.definition: Cruise control system maintaining safe distance from vehicles ahead

concepts.label: Autonomous Vehicle
concepts.definition: Vehicle capable of sensing environment and operating without human input

concepts.label: ADAS
concepts.definition: Advanced Driver Assistance Systems helping drivers with safety features

concepts.label: Lane Keeping Assist
concepts.definition: System automatically steering vehicle to stay within lane markings

concepts.label: Blind Spot Monitoring
concepts.definition: System alerting driver of vehicles in blind spot areas

concepts.label: Automatic Emergency Braking
concepts.definition: System automatically applying brakes to prevent or reduce collision impact

concepts.label: Parking Assist System
concepts.definition: System helping driver park vehicle using sensors and automated steering

concepts.label: Vehicle-to-Vehicle Communication
concepts.definition: Wireless communication system allowing vehicles to exchange information

concepts.label: Traffic Sign Recognition
concepts.definition: System using cameras to identify and interpret road signs

concepts.label: Night Vision System
concepts.definition: System using infrared technology to detect objects in dark conditions

concepts.label: Head-Up Display
concepts.definition: Display projecting information onto windshield for driver visibility

concepts.label: Suspension System
concepts.definition: Vehicle system connecting wheels to chassis and absorbing road impacts

concepts.label: MacPherson Strut
concepts.definition: Front suspension design combining shock absorber and spring in single unit

concepts.label: Double Wishbone Suspension
concepts.definition: Suspension using two wishbone-shaped arms for wheel control

concepts.label: Independent Suspension
concepts.definition: Suspension allowing each wheel to move independently of others

concepts.label: Air Suspension
concepts.definition: Suspension system using air springs instead of conventional springs

concepts.label: Shock Absorber
concepts.definition: Hydraulic device dampening suspension movement and vibrations

concepts.label: Stabilizer Bar
concepts.definition: Torsion bar reducing vehicle body roll during cornering

concepts.label: Coil Spring
concepts.definition: Helical spring supporting vehicle weight and absorbing impacts

concepts.label: Leaf Spring
concepts.definition: Simple spring using layered metal strips for heavy-duty applications

concepts.label: Ball Joint
concepts.definition: Spherical bearing connecting suspension control arms to steering knuckles

concepts.label: Steering System
concepts.definition: Vehicle system allowing driver to control direction of travel

concepts.label: Power Steering
concepts.definition: System assisting driver steering effort using hydraulic or electric power

concepts.label: Electric Power Steering
concepts.definition: Steering assistance system using electric motor instead of hydraulic pump

concepts.label: Rack and Pinion Steering
concepts.definition: Steering mechanism using pinion gear moving toothed rack

concepts.label: Steering Wheel
concepts.definition: Circular control device for driver to direct vehicle movement

concepts.label: Tie Rod
concepts.definition: Connection between steering rack and steering knuckle for wheel turning

concepts.label: Steering Column
concepts.definition: Shaft connecting steering wheel to steering gear mechanism

concepts.label: Ackermann Steering
concepts.definition: Steering geometry ensuring all wheels follow proper turning radius

concepts.label: Four Wheel Steering
concepts.definition: System allowing both front and rear wheels to steer for improved maneuverability

concepts.label: Variable Ratio Steering
concepts.definition: Steering system with changing gear ratio for different driving conditions

concepts.label: Brake System
concepts.definition: Vehicle system for slowing or stopping motion using friction

concepts.label: Disc Brake
concepts.definition: Brake system using brake pads clamping rotating disc for stopping power

concepts.label: Drum Brake
concepts.definition: Brake system using brake shoes pressing against inside of rotating drum

concepts.label: Brake Pad
concepts.definition: Friction material pressed against brake disc to create stopping force

concepts.label: Brake Fluid
concepts.definition: Hydraulic fluid transmitting force from brake pedal to brake components

concepts.label: Master Cylinder
concepts.definition: Primary component converting brake pedal force to hydraulic pressure

concepts.label: Brake Caliper
concepts.definition: Component holding brake pads and pressing them against brake disc

concepts.label: Brake Booster
concepts.definition: Device amplifying brake pedal force for easier braking

concepts.label: Parking Brake
concepts.definition: Secondary brake system for keeping vehicle stationary when parked

concepts.label: Brake Rotor
concepts.definition: Metal disc attached to wheel hub for disc brake system

concepts.label: Transmission Fluid
concepts.definition: Specialized fluid for lubrication and hydraulic operation of transmissions

concepts.label: Engine Oil
concepts.definition: Lubricant reducing friction and cooling internal combustion engine components

concepts.label: Coolant
concepts.definition: Fluid circulating through engine to maintain optimal operating temperature

concepts.label: Radiator
concepts.definition: Heat exchanger cooling engine coolant using airflow

concepts.label: Water Pump
concepts.definition: Pump circulating coolant through engine cooling system

concepts.label: Thermostat
concepts.definition: Temperature-controlled valve regulating coolant flow in engine

concepts.label: Oil Filter
concepts.definition: Filter removing contaminants from engine oil for protection

concepts.label: Air Filter
concepts.definition: Filter cleaning air entering engine for combustion

concepts.label: Fuel Filter
concepts.definition: Filter removing impurities from fuel before reaching engine

concepts.label: Timing Belt
concepts.definition: Belt synchronizing crankshaft and camshaft rotation in engine

concepts.label: Exhaust System
concepts.definition: System collecting and directing engine exhaust gases away from vehicle

concepts.label: Muffler
concepts.definition: Device reducing noise from engine exhaust gases

concepts.label: Exhaust Manifold
concepts.definition: Component collecting exhaust gases from engine cylinders

concepts.label: Oxygen Sensor
concepts.definition: Sensor measuring oxygen content in exhaust for fuel mixture control

concepts.label: EGR Valve
concepts.definition: Exhaust Gas Recirculation valve reducing nitrogen oxide emissions

concepts.label: Particulate Filter
concepts.definition: Filter capturing soot particles from diesel engine exhaust

concepts.label: Selective Catalytic Reduction
concepts.definition: Emission control technology reducing nitrogen oxides using ammonia

concepts.label: Emission Control System
concepts.definition: Integrated system reducing harmful exhaust emissions from vehicles

concepts.label: Exhaust Gas Temperature
concepts.definition: Measurement of temperature in vehicle exhaust system

concepts.label: Backpressure
concepts.definition: Resistance to exhaust gas flow in exhaust system
